description: Manage zone interface elements. If the interface name is not given then the command operates on all interfaces.

6.0.reason: Not listed in the official FLAC3D 6.0 command indexes.

7.0.description: Manage zone interface elements. If the interface name is not given then the command operates on all interfaces.

keywords.name: delete
keywords.description: All interface elements associated with this interface within the selected range are deleted.

keywords.name: extra
keywords.description: sets the extra interface element variable value for array index i. The standard [interfaceelementblock] list is available to modify this value in space.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: Add group name s to the interface elements in the range. Use of the group logic is described in Groups. This command may take the form zone interface element "slotname = groupname" — quotation marks required, spaces at equals sign ignored — where slotname is the slot assignment and groupname is the group name.

keywords.name: group-remove
keywords.description: Remove s from interface elements. If a slot is not specified, the group name is removed from all slots it is found in.

keywords.name: group-slot
keywords.description: Assign the group to the slot named s. The default slot name is Default.

keywords.name: list-extra
keywords.description: Lists the values of the interface element extra variable for array index i.

keywords.name: list-information
keywords.description: Nodes, area and normal vector of all elements in the range are displayed.

keywords.name: maximum-edge
keywords.description: f is the maximum edge length for interface elements in the range. The interface is subdivided until all of these elements have an edge length smaller than the assigned value. By default, two triangular elements are created for each quadrilateral zone face.

keywords.name: add
keywords.description: Add the specified value to the existing value.

keywords.name: gradient
keywords.description: Apply a gradient to the scalar-value provided.

keywords.name: multiply
keywords.description: Multiply the existing value by the specified value.

keywords.name: vary
keywords.description: Apply a linear variation to the scalar-value provided.

keywords.name: delete
keywords.description: All interface elements associated with this interface within the selected range are deleted.

keywords.name: extra
keywords.description: sets the extra interface element variable value for array index i. The standard [interfaceelementblock] list is available to modify this value in space.

keywords.name: group-slot
keywords.description: Add group name s to the interface elements in the range. Use of the group logic is described in Groups . This command may take the form zone interface element "slotname = groupname" — quotation marks required, spaces at equals sign ignored — where slotname is the slot assignment and groupname is the group name. An interface element may belong to many groups in different slots, up to a maximum of 128. The group range element can be used to select objects by group name. The following optional keywords are available: Remove s from interface elements. If a slot is not specified, the group name is removed from all slots it is found in. Assign the group to the slot named s . The default slot name is Default .

keywords.name: list-information
keywords.description: Lists the values of the interface element extra variable for array index i . Nodes, area (in 3D) or length (in 2D), and normal vector of all elements in the range are displayed.

keywords.name: maximum-edge
keywords.description: f is the maximum edge length for interface elements in the range. The interface is subdivided until all of these elements have an edge length smaller than the assigned value. By default, two triangular elements are created for each quadrilateral zone face in FLAC3D. In FLAC2D, a single line element is created for each zone face.

keywords.name: add
keywords.description: Add the specified value to the existing value.

keywords.name: gradient
keywords.description: Apply a gradient to the scalar-value provided. Note the origin keyword is optional, however, the default value (0,0,0) is used if the origin keyword is not included in the command — which may not produce the intended result.

keywords.name: multiply
keywords.description: Multiply the existing value by the specified value.

keywords.name: vary
keywords.description: Apply a linear variation to the scalar-value provided.
